skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Review pull requests and code changes for quality, security, performance, testing, documentation, and maintainability.
skill.summary: Guide structured code reviews with practical feedback across quality, security, tests, and design.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported code execution, XSS, credential, network, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are markdown examples, review checklist text, or reference links, with no executable skill code or prompt injection attempt. The skill is low risk and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The detected innerHTML assignment, SQL query string, hardcoded API key, environment variable access, and multiple code blocks appear inside examples that teach reviewers what to flag. They are not executed by the skill and do not request secrets, network calls, or shell access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are visibly contained in fenced markdown examples or checklist guidance. The skill metadata only allows Read, Grep, and Glob, so there is no direct execution path in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: External Reference Links Are Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public code review, OWASP, and book reference resources. No evidence found of data exfiltration, tracking endpoints, or instructions to send repository content to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are presented as references and are not embedded in executable code. Their surrounding section does not instruct the assistant to transmit data.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Lexical False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary words such as description, design, and descriptive. The reconnaissance alerts correspond to checklist text and a safe parameterized SQL example, not system probing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic implementation or system reconnaissance instructions. The only SQL-related text is a vulnerability example paired with a safe parameterized query.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss security, testing, and maintainability issues when feedback is unstructured. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical review checks and clear feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews pull requests for functionality, security, performance, tests, and documentation.
Structures feedback by severity, including critical issues and nice-to-have improvements.
Checks common vulnerabilities such as SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets, and missing authorization.
Evaluates code organization, naming, abstractions, error handling, and maintainability.
Suggests clearer tests, better documentation, and practical refactoring opportunities.
limitations: It does not run tests, linters, or security scanners by itself.
It depends on the code and context provided to the assistant.
It cannot guarantee that every bug or vulnerability is found.
It is a review guide, not a replacement for human approval on high-risk changes.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests Before Merge
use_cases.description: Check changed files for bugs, missing tests, security gaps, and maintainability concerns before approving a pull request.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Feedback
use_cases.description: Use one review structure so feedback is specific, respectful, and prioritized across reviewers.

use_cases.title: Audit Sensitive Code Paths
use_cases.description: Focus review attention on authentication, authorization, input validation, data protection, and dependency risks.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Small Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a focused diff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code change for correctness, readability, and missing tests. List findings by severity and include specific file references.

prompt_templates.title: Check Pull Request Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request using the code-review skill. Check architecture, error handling, performance, tests, and documentation. Separate blocking issues from suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a security-focused review of this change. Look for input validation, authorization, injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets, logging risks, and dependency concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Review a High-Risk Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across design and regression risk.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this refactor for behavior changes, architecture consistency, test coverage, migration risk, performance impact, and rollback concerns. Prioritize findings with evidence.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes user authentication and session handling.
output: Critical: Session invalidation is missing after password reset, which can leave old sessions active.
Important: Add tests for expired sessions and invalid reset tokens.
Suggestion: Move token validation into a small helper to reduce duplicated checks.

output_examples.input: Review a data access layer change for performance and maintainability.
output: Important: The new query can create repeated database calls for each item in the result list.
Important: The repository method needs an error case test for missing records.
Suggestion: Rename the method to describe the returned data more clearly.

output_examples.input: Review frontend code that renders user-provided profile text.
output: Critical: User-provided profile content is rendered as HTML and can create XSS risk.
Important: Add a test that verifies unsafe markup is escaped.
Suggestion: Keep display formatting in a small renderer component.

best_practices: Provide the pull request goal, changed files, and any known risk areas before asking for review.
Ask for severity-ranked findings with specific evidence and practical fixes.
Combine this skill with tests, linters, and security scanners for higher confidence.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for vague approval without providing code, context, or review goals.
Do not treat style preferences as blocking issues unless they affect maintainability.
Do not use the skill as the only review step for sensitive production changes.

faq.question: Can this skill review pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to review pull requests for correctness, security, performance, tests, and documentation.

faq.question: Does it run code or tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides review guidance and feedback, but it does not execute tests or commands.

faq.question: Can it find security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can help identify common security issues, including injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets, and authorization gaps.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It also covers naming, design, maintainability, performance, documentation, and test quality.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the diff, pull request goal, affected files, test results, and any areas where review depth matters.

faq.question: Does it replace human reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It helps reviewers work more consistently, but humans should approve important changes.
